summary: eVTOL/Urban Air Mobility sector forecast: 29.6-35.3% CAGR through 2030 (conservative range, $4.36-4.67B TAM) to 54.9% CAGR (optimistic). Archer's core opportunity: airport shuttle (32.4% CAGR, United 200-unit order), defense/military ($12.7B market, 9.5% CAGR, $142M USAF contract), and intercity segments. Archer production targets 252 units (2027), 650 units (2028), implying 5-10% market share by 2030. 12-month horizon critical gates: FAA Type Certification Basis, 400+ Wh/kg battery validation, commercial operations launch announcement.

rows.label: eVTOL Global Market TAM & CAGR (2025-2030)
rows.value: $4.36B (2030) at 29.65% CAGR; Range: $4.36-4.67B (conservative) to $28.6B (optimistic) at 35.3-54.9% CAGR

extras.metric: Global eVTOL Aircraft Market Revenue (USD billions) and CAGR
extras.rationale: Triangulated across three independent market research firms (MarketsandMarkets, Grand View Research, Mordor Intelligence); conservative estimate (29.65% CAGR) reflects risk of FAA certification delays, battery supply constraints, and infrastructure buildout. Optimistic estimate (54.9% CAGR) assumes aggressive production scaling and regulatory acceleration. Archer's revenue and profitability scale directly with sector TAM penetration and share capture.
data_sources: Grand View Research: $4.67B by 2030 at 35.3% CAGR

rows.label: Passenger Transport (Airport Shuttle) Segment CAGR & TAM
rows.value: 32.4% CAGR (2026-2030); Urban Air Mobility TAM: $4.6B (2024) → $23.5B (2030) at 31.2% CAGR

extras.metric: Airport Shuttle Segment Revenue (USD billions) and CAGR; Per-trip pricing
extras.rationale: Highest-growth near-term segment; directly aligns with United Airlines partnership (200-unit conditional order, operations in Chicago and NYC). Pricing ranges $75-240 per trip (20-40 mile airport route) implies $150-300M annual operations revenue potential once 50+ flights/day/hub is achieved. Per A2.3, commercial revenue launch is Archer's primary TSR driver. Fleet requirement: 930 eVTOLs for 17,434 passengers/day in single market indicates significant capacity opportunity.

rows.label: Defense/Military Market Opportunity & USAF Allocation
rows.value: Military segment: $12.78B (2024), 9.5% CAGR; 18% of global eVTOL market; 2,150+ units DoD allocation; Archer USAF contract: $142M (up to 6 aircraft pilot)

extras.metric: Military eVTOL Market Revenue (USD billions), DoD Unit Allocation, Archer Contract Value
extras.rationale: Defense revenue is less price-sensitive and has longer contract values than commercial operations, reducing volume volatility. USAF Agility Prime program evaluated 2,150+ units for reconnaissance, logistics, border patrol, surveillance, rescue. If USAF approves follow-on orders at 5-10% of allocation (100-200 units over 2027-2030) and Archer captures 30-50% of DoD procurement, that represents 30-100 units. Archer delivered first Midnight to USAF July 2024, validating technology and use case. However, defense volume unlikely to exceed 100-150 units/year at peak, vs. 500+ units/year potential from airport shuttle.

rows.label: Archer Production Capacity Targets & Market Share Projection
rows.value: Archer: 252 units (2027), 650 units (2028); Industry: 5,280 units by 2035; Archer implied market share: 5-10% by 2030

extras.metric: eVTOL Production Capacity (units) and Market Share (%)
extras.rationale: Archer guidance (252/2027, 650/2028) is contingent on FAA Type Certification by 2027-2028 (currently on track per A2.3, but subject to ~50 remaining FAA rules). Joby targets 48 units/year by 2027 (4/month). XPeng (China) 5,000-10,000 units annually at scale. If industry reaches 5,280 units by 2035 and Archer maintains 252+ units/year through 2027-2030, market share would be 5-10% (assuming parity with Joby). Risk: FAA Type Cert slip or United order delays would compress runway and reduce share assumptions. Marked ASSUMED because it depends on multiple unresolved near-term gates (FAA approval, battery validation, customer order execution).
extras.production_risk: Any FAA Type Cert slip >6 months compresses 2027 target significantly

rows.label: Leisure/Tourism & Adjacent Use Cases TAM
rows.value: Leisure segment growth: 200%+ by 2030; Scenic flight pricing: $100-300 per person per trip (15-30 min); EHang 15,000+ passengers in 2024-2025

extras.metric: Leisure eVTOL Market Growth Rate and Scenic Flight Pricing
extras.rationale: Leisure is not a stated priority in A2.3's near-term revenue thesis (12-month focus on USAF and United). Limited Western market data; extrapolated from EHang's China operations. However, represents hedge opportunity if USAF or United timelines slip. Archer's Midnight (4-passenger, low-noise) is suitable for scenic tours at resort destinations (Las Vegas, Hawaii, South Florida). If 5-10% of production capacity (25-65 units by 2030) is dedicated to tour operators, could yield $50-150M aircraft sales plus recurring operational revenue. Growth trajectory of 200% by 2030 suggests emerging but unproven market; confidence set to LOW due to geographic and regulatory variance.
extras.revenue_potential: $50-150M in aircraft sales + recurring operational revenue
extras.geographic_maturity: Proven in China; Western market nascent
